title_en: Taharah in Al-Iqna: Hanbali Purification Law
title_id: Taharah dalam Al-Iqna': Hukum Bersuci Hanbali
content_id:
Al-Hajjawi menyajikan hukum bersuci mazhab Hanbali dalam Al-Iqna' dengan kejelasan dan ketepatan yang sesuai untuk teks tingkat menengah yang sekaligus bersifat edukatif dan praktis. Posisi-posisi mazhab tentang air, wudhu, ghusl, dan tayammum disajikan sebagai hukum-hukum yang telah mapan berdasarkan praktik Nabi.

Pembahasan mazhab Hanbali tentang air dimulai dengan ambang batas dua kulah. Air yang volumennya kurang dari dua kulah menjadi najis jika ada najis fisik yang jatuh ke dalamnya, terlepas dari apakah kualitas yang dapat dirasakan berubah. Al-Hajjawi menyajikan ini sebagai posisi Hanbali, yang diambil dari laporan Nabi 'Ketika air mencapai dua kulah maka tidak membawa najis.' Ambang batas ini membedakan pendekatan Hanbali (dan Syafi'i) dari pendekatan Maliki, yang menggunakan perubahan kualitas yang dapat dirasakan sebagai kriteria tanpa mempertimbangkan volume.

Wudhu dalam Al-Iqna' mengikuti unsur-unsur wajib mazhab Hanbali: membasuh wajah (termasuk mulut dan hidung, yang oleh mazhab Hanbali diperlakukan sebagai bagian dari wajah untuk tujuan ini), membasuh lengan hingga dan termasuk siku, mengusap seluruh kepala (termasuk telinga), dan membasuh kaki hingga dan termasuk mata kaki. Al-Hajjawi mencatat persyaratan Hanbali bahwa mulut dan hidung harus termasuk dalam membasuh wajah sebagai unsur fardhu — sebuah posisi Hanbali yang khas yang sejalan dengan mazhab Hanafi pada poin spesifik ini.

Perbuatan-perbuatan sunnah wudhu dalam mazhab Hanbali meliputi: memulai dengan Basmallah, menggunakan siwak, membasuh tangan hingga pergelangan tangan sebelum memulai wudhu, berkumur dan memasukkan air ke hidung (sebelum membasuh wajah, karena mazhab Hanbali memperlakukannya sebagai bagian dari wajah daripada perbuatan terpisah), menyela-nyela janggut, menyela-nyela jari-jari tangan dan kaki, dan memulai setiap perbuatan dari sisi kanan.

Ghusl dalam Al-Iqna' memerlukan niat dan pembasuhan seluruh tubuh secara menyeluruh. Mazhab Hanbali — tidak seperti mazhab Hanafi — tidak mengklasifikasikan berkumur dan memasukkan air ke hidung sebagai unsur wajib ghusl yang terpisah dari persyaratan keseluruhan untuk membasuh tubuh; melainkan, mulut dan hidung adalah bagian dari tubuh yang harus dibasuh. Al-Hajjawi menyajikan metode ghusl yang lengkap sebagai tindakan yang terintegrasi.

Tayammum dalam Al-Iqna' diperbolehkan ketika air tidak tersedia atau ketika penggunaannya akan menimbulkan bahaya. Mazhab Hanbali mensyaratkan menepukkan tanah yang bersih (atau sesuatu yang memiliki kualitas tanah) dua kali: sekali untuk wajah, sekali untuk tangan hingga pergelangan tangan. Al-Hajjawi mencatat bahwa tanah harus bersih dan bahwa wajah dan tangan harus sepenuhnya tertutup dalam penepukan — kedua persyaratan tersebut mencerminkan perhatian mazhab Hanbali terhadap spesifikasi Nabi.

Bagian najasah dalam Al-Iqna' mencakup kategori-kategori utama najis dan cara pensuciannya. Posisi mazhab Hanbali tentang air liur anjing mensyaratkan pencucian tujuh kali, salah satunya dengan tanah — konsisten dengan mazhab Syafi'i dan berbeda dari posisi Maliki dan Hanafi. Al-Hajjawi menyajikan ini sebagai posisi Hanbali yang mapan berdasarkan hadis Nabi tentang mencuci wadah yang disentuh anjing.

Nilai praktis dari bab-bab taharah Al-Iqna' terletak tepat pada kejelasan dan kegunaannya. Para pelajar yang menguasai materi ini memiliki fondasi yang dapat diandalkan untuk analisis yang lebih terperinci yang akan mereka temui kemudian di Kashshaf al-Qina', dan para praktisi dapat menggunakan Al-Iqna' langsung sebagai panduan hukum bersuci Hanbali.
content_en:
Al-Hajjawi presents the Hanbali school's purification law in Al-Iqna with the clarity and precision appropriate for an intermediate-level text that is both educational and practical. The school's positions on water, ablution, ghusl, and tayammum are presented as settled rulings grounded in the prophetic practice.

The Hanbali school's treatment of water begins with the two-qullah threshold. Water less than two qullahs in volume becomes impure if any physical impurity falls into it, regardless of whether its perceptible qualities change. Al-Hajjawi presents this as the Hanbali position, derived from the prophetic report 'When water reaches two qullahs it carries no impurity.' This threshold distinguishes the Hanbali (and Shafi'i) approach from the Maliki approach, which uses change in perceptible qualities as the criterion regardless of volume.

Wudu' in Al-Iqna follows the Hanbali school's obligatory elements: washing the face (including the mouth and nose, which the Hanbali school treats as part of the face for this purpose), washing the arms to and including the elbows, wiping the entire head (including the ears), and washing the feet to and including the ankles. Al-Hajjawi notes the Hanbali requirement that the mouth and nose be included in washing the face as a fard element — a distinctive Hanbali position that aligns with the Hanafi school on this specific point.

The Hanbali school's sunnah acts in wudu' include: beginning with the Basmala, using the miswak (tooth-stick), washing the hands up to the wrists before beginning the ablution, rinsing the mouth and nostrils (before face-washing, as the Hanbali school treats them as part of the face rather than separate acts), running the fingers through the beard, running fingers between the fingers and toes, and beginning each act with the right side.

Ghusl in Al-Iqna requires intention and complete washing of the entire body. The Hanbali school — unlike the Hanafi school — does not classify rinsing the mouth and nostrils as separate obligatory elements of ghusl distinct from the overall requirement to wash the body; rather, the mouth and nose are part of the body that must be washed. Al-Hajjawi presents the complete method of ghusl as an integrated act.

Tayammum in Al-Iqna is permitted when water is unavailable or when its use would cause harm. The Hanbali school requires striking clean earth (or something with the qualities of earth) twice: once for the face, once for the hands to the wrists. Al-Hajjawi notes that the earth must be clean and that the face and hands must be completely covered in the striking — both requirements reflecting the Hanbali school's attention to prophetic specifications.

The najasah (ritual impurity) section of Al-Iqna covers the major categories of impurity and their purification. The Hanbali school's position on dog saliva requires sevenfold washing, one with earth — consistent with the Shafi'i school and different from the Maliki and Hanafi positions. Al-Hajjawi presents this as the established Hanbali position based on the prophetic hadith on washing containers touched by dogs.

The practical value of Al-Iqna's taharah chapters lies precisely in their clarity and usability. Students who mastered this material had a reliable foundation for the more detailed analysis they would later encounter in Kashshaf al-Qina, and practitioners could use Al-Iqna directly as a guide to Hanbali purification rulings.
content_ar: Al-Hajjawi presents the الحنبلي school's purification law in Al-Iqna with the clarity and precision appropriate for an intermediate-level text that is both educational and practical. The school's positions on water, ablution, الغسل, and التيمم are presented as settled rulings grounded in the prophetic practice. The الحنبلي school's treatment of water begins with the two-qullah threshold. Water less than two qullahs in volume becomes impure إذا any physical impurity falls into it, regardless of whether its perceptible qualities change. Al-Hajjawi presents this as the الحنبلي position, derived from the prophetic report 'عندما water reaches two qullahs it carries no impurity.' This threshold distinguishes the الحنبلي (and الشافعي) approach from the المالكي approach, which uses change in perceptible qualities as the criterion regardless of volume. الوضوء' in Al-Iqna follows the الحنبلي school's obligatory elements: washing the face (بما في ذلك the mouth and nose, which the الحنبلي school treats as part of the face for this purpose), washing the arms to and بما في ذلك the elbows, wiping the entire head (بما في ذلك the ears), and washing the feet to and بما في ذلك the ankles. Al-Hajjawi notes the الحنبلي requirement that the mouth and nose be included in washing the face as a الفرض element — a distinctive الحنبلي position that aligns with the الحنفي school on this specific point. The الحنبلي school's السنة acts in الوضوء' include: beginning with the Basmala, using the miswak (tooth-stick), washing the hands up to the wrists قبل beginning the ablution, rinsing the mouth and nostrils (قبل face-washing, as the الحنبلي school treats them as part of the face rather than separate acts), running the fingers through the beard, running fingers between the fingers and toes, and beginning each act with the الحق side. الغسل in Al-Iqna requires intention and complete washing of the entire الجسد. The الحنبلي school — unlike the الحنفي school — does not classify rinsing the mouth and nostrils as separate obligatory elements of الغسل distinct from the overall requirement to wash the الجسد; rather, the mouth and nose are part of the الجسد that must be washed. Al-Hajjawi presents the complete method of الغسل as an integrated act. التيمم in Al-Iqna is permitted عندما water is unavailable or عندما its use would cause harm. The الحنبلي school requires striking clean earth (or something with the qualities of earth) twice: حين for the face, حين for the hands to the wrists. Al-Hajjawi notes that the earth must be clean and that the face and hands must be completely covered in the striking — both requirements reflecting the الحنبلي school's attention to prophetic specifications. The النجاسة (ritual impurity) الفصل of Al-Iqna covers the major categories of impurity and their purification.
